message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="kent-beck" color="green" summary="Agreed on captured_at and collaborative TDD/domain modeling approach">
Scott, perfect alignment. Two quick notes:

**1. Yes on `captured_at`.** Good catch. And it highlights something I care about — the test should assert something *meaningful* about the domain, not just "did I set a field." The fact that GTD cares about *when* things entered the system (for the weekly review: "how long has this been sitting in your inbox?") makes `captured_at` a domain concept, not just bookkeeping. Your instinct to add it is domain-driven, not over-engineering. I like it.

**2. Your role in the TDD cycle is exactly what you described.** You provide the domain vocabulary and state machine sketch as a hypothesis. I sequence the tests. The types emerge from making tests pass. When the tests surprise us — and they will — we adjust the model. That's the collaborative tension between domain modeling and TDD that produces the best designs.

We're in good shape. Waiting on Marty's scope definition so we can start sequencing the actual implementation tasks.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Kent and I are in full agreement. Nothing more to add -- he confirmed the collaborative model between domain architecture and TDD that we'll use during implementation. The `captured_at` timestamp is a domain concept (review asks "how long has this been in your inbox?"), not just bookkeeping. Good validation.

All my reviewer exchanges are now complete:

| Teammate | Status | Key Agreements |
|----------|--------|----------------|
| Marty (Driver) | CONSENT sent | All 4 open questions answered, scope concern flagged |
| Kent | Aligned | TDD sequencing, "stalled" term, types emerge from tests, captured_at |
| Luca | Aligned | New tables, TEXT discriminators, transient processing, unified actions table |
| Carson | Aligned | HTMX partial swaps, transient processing state, context filtering |
| Steve Schoger | Aligned | Card-based processing, stalled project treatment, inbox badge weight |
| Steve Krug | Aligned | Wizard over form, phased delivery, contexts in Phase 1 |
| Lea | Aligned | CSS component modifiers map to domain types, sticky headers, details/summary |
| Heydon | Awaiting response | Sent a11y analysis (multi-list nav, context grouping, processing flow) |

I'm ready and waiting. No further action needed from me until Heydon responds or the team lead collects consensus.
